title: Safe Censoring
text: necessary times censor certain characters string. case, censoring simply means replacing set characters different set meaningless characters length. example, might wish censor substring “low” phrase, “Flowers friend.” result “F***ers friend.” Given goal preserving length, accomplished working fixed search strings using mgsub. However, can breakdown using variable length regular expression matching. number censor characters replacement based length regular expression, match . fails maintain character length. Even fixed matches, shouldn’t necessary produce maintain equivalent vector censor replacment.
code: library(mgsub) string = "Time to flip this family into a fun pit of pudding!" pattern = c("flip", "family", "fun") replacement = vapply(pattern, function(x) {   paste(rep("*", nchar(x)), collapse = "") }, FUN.VALUE = "") mgsub(string, pattern, replacement) ## [1] "Time to **** this ****** into a *** pit of pudding!" string = "Time to flip this family into a fun pit of pudding!" pattern = c("f[^ ]*i[^ ]*", "fun") replacement = vapply(pattern, function(x) {   paste(rep("*", nchar(x)), collapse = "") }, FUN.VALUE = "") mgsub(string, pattern, replacement) ## [1] "Time to ************ this ************ into a *** pit of pudding!"

title: Safe Censoring
text: idea mgsub_censor comes . mgsub_censor provides safe, simultaneous string substitution functionality mgsub narrow task censoring strings. provide patterns match well desired censoring character censoring applied simultaneously.
code: string = "Time to flip this family into a fun pit of pudding!" pattern = c("f[^ ]*i[^ ]*", "fun") mgsub::mgsub_censor(string = string, pattern = pattern, censor = "*") ## [1] "Time to **** this ****** into a *** pit of pudding!"

title: Safe Censoring
text: may wish produce comical censoring effects often used comic strips. suppored multicharacter censors can provided multiple ways.

title: Safe Censoring
text: split argument TRUE (default case), value split individual characters sampled produce effect. randomness can limited setting seed. also possible produce output characters input setting split FALSE. case, 4 character censor replicated 4 times match length output 12 characters longer input. Use caution.
code: string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = "?#!*" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?!*! a cookie?" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?#!! a cookie?" string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = "?#!*" mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE, seed = 1002) ## [1] "Why don't you go *?!? a cookie?" string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = "?#!*" mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = FALSE) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?#!*?#!*?#!*?#!* a cookie?"

what: Single character, vector with length greater than one
title: Safe Censoring
text: case multicharacter, vector length one split = TRUE. Note setting split = FALSE doesn’t impact output character count.
code: string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = c("?", "#", "!", "*") print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go !*?# a cookie?" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = FALSE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?!*# a cookie?" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, seed = 1002)) ## [1] "Why don't you go *?!? a cookie?"

what: Multicharacter, vector with length greater than one
title: Safe Censoring
text: case, split = TRUE, fact vector length greater one doesn’t matter. vector element split, set unlisted. split set FALSE, ’s case length one, multicharacter censor except vector elements sampled. sample two 2-character elements four times end 8 characters, 4 started . Use split = FALSE caution.
code: string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = c("?#", "!*") print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go !*?# a cookie?" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE)) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?!*# a cookie?" print(mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = TRUE, seed = 1002)) ## [1] "Why don't you go *?!? a cookie?" string = "Why don't you go flip a cookie?" pattern = "flip" censor = c("?#", "!*") mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor, split = FALSE) ## [1] "Why don't you go ?#!*?#!* a cookie?"

title: Safe Censoring
text: compelling feature mgsub ’s safety. quick overview meant safety: Longer matches preferred shorter matches substitution first placeholders used accidental string collisions don’t occur mgsub_censor maintains safety demonstrated . Note shorter kilo ignored matching kilogram despite substring.
code: string = "I'm selling 100 kilograms of bleach for $20/kilo" pattern = c("kilo", "kilogram") censor = "*" mgsub::mgsub_censor(string, pattern, censor) ## [1] "I'm selling 100 ********s of bleach for $20/****"

title: Safe String Substitution
text: Modifying existing strings via substitution common practice programing. end, functions like gsub provide method accomplish . example “hey” replaced “ho” transforming line Ramones Santa Claus leaving Christmas Eve.
code: s = "hey ho, let's go!" gsub("hey", "ho", s) ## [1] "ho ho, let's go!"

title: Safe String Substitution
text: gsub supports one string matching one string replacement. means can match multiple conditions, can provide one condition replacement. construct regular expression matches “hey” “ho” replaces matches “yo”. wanted replace “hey” “get” “ho” “ready” need two steps. sequential process however can result undesired changes. want swap “hey” “ho” , can see process breaks . change happens order, “hey” becomes “ho” every “ho” becomes “hey”, undoing first step.
code: s = "hey ho, let's go!" gsub("hey|ho", "yo", s) ## [1] "yo yo, let's go!" s = "hey ho, let's go!" s_new = gsub("hey", "get", s) s_new = gsub("ho", "ready", s_new) s_new ## [1] "get ready, let's go!" s = "hey ho, let's go!" s_new = gsub("hey", "ho", s) s_new = gsub("ho", "hey", s_new) s_new ## [1] "hey hey, let's go!"

title: Safe String Substitution
text: idea mgsub comes . mgsub safe, simultaneous string substitution function. pass patterns match well replacements replacements applied simultaneously.
code: library(mgsub) s = "hey ho, let's go!" mgsub::mgsub(string = s, pattern = c("hey", "ho"), replacement = c("ho", "hey")) ## [1] "ho hey, let's go!"

title: Safe String Substitution
text: mgsub fully supports regular expressions matching criteria well backreferences replacement. Note matching criteria ignores “dopachloride” replacement matches “Dopazamine” “dopastriamine” (fake chemicals despite replace string claims!). Furthermore, can pass options gsub family. example can see fixed string matching
code: s = "Dopazamine is not the same as dopachloride or dopastriamine, yet is still fake." pattern = c("[Dd]opa([^ ]*?mine)", "fake") replacement = c("Meta\\1", "real") mgsub::mgsub(s, pattern, replacement) ## [1] "Metazamine is not the same as dopachloride or Metastriamine, yet is still real." s = "All my life I chased $money$ and .power. - not love!" pattern = c("$money$", ".power.", "love") replacement = c("balloons", "dolphins", "success") mgsub::mgsub(s, pattern, replacement, fixed = TRUE) ## [1] "All my life I chased balloons and dolphins - not success!"

title: Safe String Substitution
text: actually compelling feature mgsub. Several packages implement similar type function (also named mgsub) employ safe substitution - qdap, bazar textclean. quick overview meant safety: Longer matches preferred shorter matches substitution first placeholders used accidental string collisions don’t occur

title: Safe, Multiple, Simultaneous String Substitution
text: string want make substitutions . want make many different substitutions time want done safe way. example, want shift word “hey, ?” left replacing “hey” “”, “” “, etc. Existing functions either support vectorization (e.g. gsub), don’t support simultaneous substition (e.g. stringr::str_replace_all) replacements unsafe manner (e.g. qdap::mgsub). lightweight, pure R function dependencies avoid package bloat used.

title: Safe, Multiple, Simultaneous String Substitution
code: install.packages('mgsub') #Install the latest version from GitHub #devtools::install_github("bmewing/mgsub")

title: Safe, Multiple, Simultaneous String Substitution
text: Simply pass vector strings modified, vector patterns match vector replacements. watch safely, simultaneously replaced! pattern match supplied first replacement vector follows. Recycling make easy provide single replacement (pattern replacements) multiple matches. Matches replacements can still supplied regex expressions. Additional arguments can passed sub/gsub/gregexpr family internal functions.
code: mgsub::mgsub(string,pattern=c(),replacement=c(),recycle=FALSE,...) mgsub::mgsub("hey, how are you?",c("hey","how","are","you"),c("how","are","you","hey")) mgsub::mgsub("hey, ho, let's go!",c("hey","ho","go"),"ugh",recycle=TRUE) mgsub::mgsub("Dopazamine is not the same as Dopachloride and is still fake.",               c("[Dd]opa(.*?mine)","fake"), c("Meta\\1","real"),ignore.case=F)

title: mgsub 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-06 Rewrote key functions C provide significant performance boost (4-5x speedup certain tests) get_matches filter_overlap primary performance enhancers get_matches_base filter_overlap_base retain original base R functionality case issues building code Changed maintainer email address current one bouncing CRAN emails

title: mgsub 1.7.3
text: CRAN release: 2021-07-28 Vignettes using qdap package available causing errors. update modifies vignette remove use qdap (microbenchmark) removed ‘suggests’.

title: mgsub 1.7.2
text: CRAN release: 2020-07-22 warning surfacing newer versions R around certain functions linked documentation. technically bug, CICD pipeline setup report errors. ensure full compliance CRAN Standards, linking updated. minor code refactor apply linting standards enable easier contribution, removed superfulous lines.

title: mgsub 1.7.1
text: CRAN release: 2019-03-13 Added RNG Version control tests support future versions R. functional changes way package runs, ensure testing environment continues work expected.

title: mgsub 1.7
text: Added mgsub_censor function enables fast, simultaneous censoring patterns Trimmed unnecessary logic helper functions

title: mgsub 1.6
text: New method employed leveraging gregexpr reduce amount computation time spend identifying matches slightly improves performance. Added ‘escapes’ normal processing ’s one match replace improve performance.

title: mgsub 1.5
text: Removed NSE evalutaion, deprecating named list (dictionary) method Added support vector inputs Added NA handling Slightly improved vectorized performance Updated license (MIT)
